item.name: Checkouts
item.description:
Accept payments from your end users by adding the Checkouts model to your platform.
SumUp supports standard and single payment 3DS checkout flows.

The Checkout model allows creating, listing, retrieving, processing and deactivating checkouts.
A payment is completed by creating a checkout and then processing the checkout.

description.content: Get payment methods available for the given merchant to use with a checkout.
The amount for which the payment methods should be eligible, in major units.
The currency for which the payment methods should be eligible.
(Required) The SumUp merchant code.

response.name: Available payment methods

description.content: The amount for which the payment methods should be eligible, in major units.
The currency for which the payment methods should be eligible.
(Required) The SumUp merchant code.

response.name: The request is invalid for the submitted query parameters.

description.content: The amount for which the payment methods should be eligible, in major units.
The currency for which the payment methods should be eligible.
(Required) The SumUp merchant code.

Creates a new payment checkout resource. The unique `checkout_reference` created by this request, is used for further manipulation of the checkout.

For 3DS checkouts, add the `redirect_url` parameter to your request body schema.

Follow by processing a checkout to charge the provided payment instrument.

response.name: Returns the created checkout resource.
The request body is invalid.
The request is not authorized.
The request isn't sufficiently authorized to create a checkout.
A checkout already exists for the provided unique parameters.

description.content: Lists created checkout resources according to the applied `checkout_reference`.
Filters the list of checkout resources by the unique ID of the checkout.

response.name: Returns a list of checkout resources.

description.content: Filters the list of checkout resources by the unique ID of the checkout.

response.name: The request is not authorized.

description.content: Filters the list of checkout resources by the unique ID of the checkout.
Retrieves an identified checkout resource. Use this request after processing a checkout to confirm its status and inform the end user respectively.
(Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: Returns the requested checkout resource.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: The request is not authorized.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: The requested resource does not exist.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

Processing a checkout will attempt to charge the provided payment instrument for the amount of the specified checkout resource initiated in the `Create a checkout` endpoint.

Follow this request with `Retrieve a checkout` to confirm its status.
(Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: Returns the checkout resource after a processing attempt.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: Returns the next required action for asynchronous checkout processing.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: The request body is invalid for processing the checkout.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: The request is not authorized.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: The requested resource does not exist.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: The request conflicts with the current state of the resource.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.
Deactivates an identified checkout resource. If the checkout has already been processed it can not be deactivated.
(Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: Returns the checkout object after successful deactivation.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: The request is not authorized.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: The requested resource does not exist.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

response.name: The request conflicts with the current state of the resource.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the checkout resource.

item.name: Customers
item.description:
Allow your regular customers to save their information with the Customers model.
This will prevent re-entering payment instrument information for recurring payments on your platform.

Depending on the needs you can allow, creating, listing or deactivating payment instruments & creating, retrieving and updating customers.

description.content: Creates a new saved customer resource which you can later manipulate and save payment instruments to.

response.name: Returns the customer resource.
The request body is invalid.
The request is not authorized.
The request is authenticated but not permitted for this operation.
A customer with the provided identifier already exists.

description.content: Retrieves an identified saved customer resource through the unique `customer_id` parameter, generated upon customer creation.
(Required) Unique ID of the saved customer resource.

response.name: Returns the customer resource.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the saved customer resource.

response.name: The request is not authorized.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the saved customer resource.

response.name: The request is authenticated but not permitted for this operation.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the saved customer resource.

response.name: The requested resource does not exist.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the saved customer resource.

Updates an identified saved customer resource's personal details.

The request only overwrites the parameters included in the request, all other parameters will remain with their initially assigned values.
(Required) Unique ID of the saved customer resource.

response.name: Returns the customer resource.

item.name: Transactions
item.description:
Retrieve details for a specific transaction by it’s `id`
or any other required query parameter, or list all transactions related to the merchant account.

description.content: Refunds an identified transaction either in full or partially.
(Required) Unique ID of the transaction.

response.name: Returns an empty response body when the operation succeeds.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the transaction.

response.name: The requested resource does not exist.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the transaction.

response.name: The transaction cannot be refunded due to business constraints.

description.content: (Required) Unique ID of the transaction.

Retrieves the full details of an identified transaction. The transaction resource is identified by a query parameter and *one* of following parameters is required:

 *  `id`
 *  `internal_id`
 *  `transaction_code`
 *  `foreign_transaction_id`
 *  `client_transaction_id`
Retrieves the transaction resource with the specified transaction ID (the `id` parameter in the transaction resource).
Retrieves the transaction resource with the specified internal transaction ID (the `internal_id` parameter in the transaction resource).
Retrieves the transaction resource with the specified transaction code.
External/foreign transaction id (passed by clients).
Client transaction id.
(Required)

response.name: Returns the requested transaction resource.

description.content: Retrieves the transaction resource with the specified transaction ID (the `id` parameter in the transaction resource).
Retrieves the transaction resource with the specified internal transaction ID (the `internal_id` parameter in the transaction resource).
Retrieves the transaction resource with the specified transaction code.
External/foreign transaction id (passed by clients).
Client transaction id.
(Required)

Filters the results by the latest modification time of resources and returns only transactions that are modified *at or after* the specified timestamp (in [ISO8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format).
Filters the results by the creation time of resources and returns only transactions that are created *before* the specified timestamp (in [ISO8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format).

Filters the results by the reference ID of transaction events and returns only transactions with events whose IDs are *smaller* than the specified value. This parameters supersedes the `newest_time` parameter (if both are provided in the request).

item.name: Payouts
item.description:
The Payouts model will allow you to track funds you’ve received from SumUp.
You can receive a detailed payouts list with information like dates, fees, references and statuses, using the `List payouts` endpoint.

description.content: Lists ordered payouts for the merchant profile.
(Required) Start date (in [ISO8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format).
(Required) End date (in [ISO8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format).
(This can only be one of json,csv)
(This can only be one of desc,asc)
(Required)

response.name: Returns the list of payouts for the requested period.

description.content: (Required) Start date (in [ISO8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format).
(Required) End date (in [ISO8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format).

item.name: Receipts
item.description: The Receipts model obtains receipt-like details for specific transactions.

description.content: Retrieves receipt specific data for a transaction.
(Required) Merchant code.
The ID of the transaction event (refund).
(Required) SumUp unique transaction ID or transaction code, e.g. TS7HDYLSKD.

response.name: Returns receipt details for the requested transaction.

Returns list of operators for currently authorized user's merchant.

Search query used to filter users that match given query term.

Current implementation allow querying only over the email address.
All operators whos email address contains the query string are returned.
If true the list of operators will include also the primary user.

response.name: List of operators.

description.content:
Search query used to filter users that match given query term.

Current implementation allow querying only over the email address.
All operators whos email address contains the query string are returned.
If true the list of operators will include also the primary user.

response.name: Authentication failed or missing required scope.

item.name: Members
item.description: Endpoints to manage account members. Members are users that have membership within merchant accounts.

item.name: Memberships
item.description: Endpoints to manage user's memberships. Memberships are used to connect the user to merchant accounts and to grant them access to the merchant's resources via roles.

Offset of the first member to return.
Maximum number of members to return.
Filter memberships by resource kind.
Filter the returned memberships by the membership status. (This can only be one of accepted,pending,expired,disabled,unknown)

Filter memberships by the name of the resource the membership is in.

Filter memberships by the parent of the resource the membership is in.
When filtering by parent both `resource.parent.id` and `resource.parent.type` must be present. Pass explicit null to filter for resources without a parent.

When filtering by parent both `resource.parent.id` and `resource.parent.type` must be present. Pass explicit null to filter for resources without a parent.

item.name: Roles
item.description: Endpoints to manage custom roles. Custom roles allow you to tailor roles from individual permissions to match your needs. Once created, you can assign your custom roles to your merchant account members using the memberships.

item.name: Merchants
item.description: Merchant account represents a single business entity at SumUp.

description.content: Retrieve a merchant.

The version of the resource. At the moment, the only supported value is `latest`. When provided and the requested resource's `change_status` is pending, the resource will be returned with all pending changes applied. When no changes are pending the resource is returned as is. The `change_status` in the response body will reflect the current state of the resource.
(Required) Short unique identifier for the merchant.

Return the reader only if it has been modified after the specified timestamp given in the headers.

Timestamps are accepted in the following formats:

 - HTTP Standard: [IMF format (RFC 5322)](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5322#section-3.3), sometimes also referred to as [RFC 7231](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7231#section-7.1.1.1).
 - RFC 3339: Used for timestamps in JSON payloads on this API.

Creates a Checkout for a Reader.

This process is asynchronous and the actual transaction may take some time to be started on the device.


There are some caveats when using this endpoint:
* The target device must be online, otherwise checkout won't be accepted
* After the checkout is accepted, the system has 60 seconds to start the payment on the target device. During this time, any other checkout for the same device will be rejected.


**Note**: If the target device is a Solo, it must be in version 3.3.24.3 or higher.
(Required) Merchant Code
(Required) The unique identifier of the Reader

Provides the last known status for a Reader.

This endpoint allows you to retrieve updates from the connected card reader, including the current screen being displayed during the payment process and the device status (battery level, connectivity, and update state).

Supported States

* `IDLE` – Reader ready for next transaction
* `SELECTING_TIP` – Waiting for tip input
* `WAITING_FOR_CARD` – Awaiting card insert/tap
* `WAITING_FOR_PIN` – Waiting for PIN entry
* `WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURE` – Waiting for customer signature
* `UPDATING_FIRMWARE` – Firmware update in progress

Device Status

* `ONLINE` – Device connected and operational
* `OFFLINE` – Device disconnected (last state persisted)

**Note**: If the target device is a Solo, it must be in version 3.3.39.0 or higher.
(Required) Merchant Code
(Required) The unique identifier of the Reader

response.name: Response with the device status.

Terminate a Reader Checkout stops the current transaction on the target device.

This process is asynchronous and the actual termination may take some time to be performed on the device.


There are some caveats when using this endpoint:
* The target device must be online, otherwise terminate won't be accepted
* The action will succeed only if the device is waiting for cardholder action: e.g: waiting for card, waiting for PIN, etc.
* There is no confirmation of the termination.

If a transaction is successfully terminated and `return_url` was provided on Checkout, the transaction status will be sent as `failed` to the provided URL.


**Note**: If the target device is a Solo, it must be in version 3.3.28.0 or higher.
(Required) Merchant Code
(Required) The unique identifier of the Reader

SumUp’s REST API operates with [JSON](https://www.json.org/json-en.html) HTTP requests and responses. The request bodies are sent through resource-oriented URLs and use the standard [HTTP response codes](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTTP/Status).

You can experiment and work on your integration in a sandbox that doesn't affect your regular data and doesn't process real transactions. To create a sandbox merchant account visit the [dashboard](https://me.sumup.com/settings/developer). To use the sandbox when interacting with SumUp APIs [create an API](https://me.sumup.com/settings/api-keys) key and use it for [authentication](https://developer.sumup.com/api/authentication).
